+3V.notes: Battery positive / VDD rail (readme: programmer pad "3V0" ties Batt+ and power). CR2032 through BAT-HLD-001 sheet-metal holder; metal can is anode, PCB pad cathode path completes GND. Must star/short to U1 VDD and bulk/decoupling C1/C2. No LED series resistors — rail IR drop and battery ESR set LED current. Keep low impedance to MCU and decoupling; avoid long loops with GND.

GND.notes: System ground / battery return. Programmer pad GND. Decoupling C1/C2 return here. Minimize +3V–GND loop area under U1 and around battery. LED returns go through charlieplex matrix (not a shared LED GND plane).

CPX-0.notes: Charlieplex line 0 (U1 PA2). One of ten matrix rows/cols driving 0402 LEDs. High peak GPIO current, no series R; length-match preference to other CPX lines for uniform brightness. Keep clear of MIC analog path.

CPX-1.notes: Charlieplex line 1 (U1 PA4). Same class as CPX-0; matrix interconnect to LED ring.

MIC.notes: Amplified MEMS mic output to STM8L 12-bit ADC (U1 PD0). Audio-reactive firmware mode depends on clean samples. Keep short, away from CPX switching and digital edges; do not run under LED ring currents. MK1 at (0, -4.6) mm toward hook side.

SDA.notes: I2C data (U1 PC0 ↔ U2). 10k pull-up R1 to +3V when IMU populated. Keep matched length with SCL; route as a pair away from CPX if IMU fitted.

SCL.notes: I2C clock (U1 PC1 ↔ U2). 10k pull-up R2 to +3V when IMU populated. Length-match SDA.

XL-INT1.notes: LSM6DSM INT1 → U1 PD4. Wake/interrupt from IMU. Optional (IMU often unpopulated). Prefer short path U2→U1; low priority vs power/CPX/MIC.

XL-INT2.notes: LSM6DSM INT2 → U1 PC4. Optional second IMU interrupt.

SW-A.notes: Button side A (U1 PB1 ↔ S1). Mode change on press; 500 ms hold → deep sleep (~15 uA). Short path; place switch at edge for nail access (product: S1 at x=-4.25).

SW-B.notes: Button side B (U1 PB6 ↔ S1). Mate of SW-A on KXT3 footprint.

TX.notes: Serial out (readme: GPIO PB2 / also PC3 net). Programmer pad TX. Factory/test only; length secondary to pogo pad placement for balanced spring force.

RX.notes: Serial in (readme: GPIO PB4 / also PC2 net). Programmer pad RX. Mate with TX pad geometry.

Net-(R3-Pad2).notes: NRST node: U1 ~NRST, 10k pull-up R3 to +3V, programmer pad RST (TP5). Active-low reset. Keep short, clean pull-up; do not couple to CPX edges.

Net-(TP2-Pad1).notes: SWIM programming interface (U1 SWIM pin ↔ TP2). STLink-V2 SWIM flashing of STM8L. Critical for manufacturing; pad placement fixed by programmer jig geometry.

fixed.notes: STM8L151G4 UFQFPN28 — center of board; main compute + charlieplex driver.
BAT-HLD-001-HALO CR2032 holder — stacked on center; anode can / cathode pad.
Earring hook/eyelet — top of product, extends ~2 mm beyond 24 mm disk.
KXT3 tactile — edge placement for nail actuation.
SPW2430 MEMS mic — acoustic port orientation fixed by case/face.
LSM6DSM IMU footprint (often DNP). Keep for optional populate.
Decoupling cap (1 uF or 0.1 uF class) near U1 — +3V/GND loop.

regions.name: core_electronics
regions.notes: Center island — MCU, battery, decoupling, mic, button, optional IMU. Prefer short power and reset here.

regions.name: led_ring
regions.notes: Annular zone for D1–D90 at ~11 mm radius, 4° spacing, cathodes toward center. CPX routing fans from U1 into this ring. LEDs are geometric product constraints (usually locked by construction scripts; re-place only with polar constraints).

regions.name: programmer_rim
regions.notes: ⌀1 mm copper pogo pads balanced around board for spring-pin programmer.

config.notes: HALO-90 wearable LED earring. Dense circular layout: MCU + battery at center, 90 LEDs on a 4° ring (cathodes toward center), programming pogo pads, optional LSM6DSM IMU. Power is severely constrained (CR2032, GPIO-driven LEDs with no series resistors). Charlieplex CPX lines carry pulsed LED current and switch at >1 kHz — treat as high-edge-rate / EMI-sensitive. Keep analog MIC and I2C quiet relative to CPX. Mechanicals (battery holder, hook, tooling holes, pogo pads) are fixed by product geometry.
